title: Principal Applied Scientist / Lounge by Zalando (all genders)
content:
**THE ROLE & TEAM**  
Lounge by Zalando is the leading online outlet in Europe, offering limited-time exclusive discounts on premium fashion and lifestyle brands. In this role, you’ll work across the team that builds the customers facing fashion assistant, and personalized landing page.  
You’ll work across two teams, focusing on recommendation systems and agentic development. As the principle Applied Scientist, you’ll define the evals which measure success, and get hands on both custom ML models and LLM based solutions with our engineers.  
This role is an IC role, we expect you to have great coding skills, and be able to contribute to production like a software engineer, at a fast pace.

**INCLUSIVE BY DESIGN**

If you think you have what it takes, we encourage you to apply even if you don't meet every single requirement. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles! At Zalando, our vision is to be the leading pan-European ecosystem for fashion and lifestyle e-commerce – one that thrives on diversity and is truly inclusive by design. We believe that diverse teams fuel innovation and creativity, and we actively seek out talent from all backgrounds. We actively seek to reduce bias in our hiring and employment processes, focusing on your qualifications, skills, and contributions. To support this, we kindly ask that you refrain from including personal details such as your photo, age, or marital status in your CV, ensuring a fair and equitable evaluation based solely on your abilities and potential.We are committed to providing an exceptional and accessible candidate experience for everyone. If you require any accommodations to support you throughout the hiring process, please let us know – we are here to assist you.

Discover more about our commitment to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace: https://jobs.zalando.com/en/our-culture/diversity-and-inclusion

**WHAT WE'D LOVE YOU TO DO (AND LOVE DOING)**

* Use LLM APIs to create the ideal fashion assistant for our customers.
* Develop and improve machine learning applications, such as personalized recommendation systems, to enhance our customers' browsing experience and communication.
* Translate ambiguous business challenges into research questions, contribute to defining solutions, and lead the end-to-end applied science strategy, including model selection, problem formulation, and approaches for training and inference. Develop and implement effective evaluation frameworks to assess the impact of initiatives, analyze customer behavior, and evaluate business performance.
* Use AI coding tools to deliver software at a fast pace.

**WE'D LOVE TO MEET YOU IF..**

* Strong expertise in production ML applications, with at least 3 years of hands-on industry experience building and deploying scalable, high-performance recommendation or search solutions.
* At least 5 years of software engineering experience using Python.
* LLM application development and evaluation is a highly desired bo

# Principal AI Engineer - Conversational Banking

Department:Tech - Assistance

Location:Berlin

## About us

N26 is Europe's leading digital bank, fully licensed in Germany and active across more than 20 markets. The mission is not simply to build another fintech product, but to build Europe's most trusted digital primary bank account — the account where people's salaries land, their bills are paid, and their financial lives are managed.
That ambition shapes how we build. As we enter a new phase of maturity, our focus is on scalability, robustness, and the kind of operational excellence that earns everyday trust — not just signups. New senior leaders have joined to drive this chapter, and we're sharpening how we ship and sustain products at scale.
We're also expanding into the moments that make a bank indispensable, from mobile connectivity to financial tools designed for families. These aren't adjacent experiments; they're part of a deliberate strategy to be embedded in daily life.

We are making a strategic investment in Artificial Intelligence and Generative AI to redefine how customers interact with N26. We are looking for a Principal AI Engineer to architect the next generation of intelligent, multi-channel conversational ecosystems and automated backend intelligence.  In this role, you will support our vision for Conversational Banking powered by AI, and building seamless automation for Operations. Serving as the ultimate technical authority and productionizing partner for our Data Science, Engineering, and Product teams, your mission is delivery optimized for velocity, reliability, and modular design. title: Senior Product Architect
content:
# Senior Product Architect

## About the Opportunity

The Customer Insights & Adoption (CIA) team is hiring a Senior Product Architect, Internal Tools (f/m/d) to design and build internal products that help Contentful teams work smarter. This role serves internal product development, turning ambiguous workflow problems into useful systems that improve how Customer Experience, Sales, and Partner-facing teams operate every day.

This is a hands-on role with approximately 60% implementation and 40% architecture and planning. You will actively contribute production code while owning technical design and direction for a focused set of internal tools that support pipeline, retention, and customer experience outcomes. You’ll prototype, iterate, and operationalize internal systems that are lightweight where they should be, durable where they need to be, and designed for clear long-term ownership.

If you are excited by the idea of shipping high-leverage internal tools, bringing order to messy workflows, and translating promising prototypes into systems people actually rely on, this role offers the chance to have visible impact across the business.

### Why this role matters

CIA’s internal tools work is part of how we turn customer, field, and partner insights into operational advantage. The right tools help our teams move faster, reduce manual work, improve data quality, and create a more consistent way of supporting customers and partners. This role exists to make those improvements real — not as one-off experiments, but as well-scoped internal products and patterns that deliver demonstrable value for our internal teams.

* Join a high-impact team building internal products that improve customer experience, strengthen field effectiveness, and help Contentful scale more effectively
* Shape how CIA approaches internal product development, R&D, and technical architecture — with meaningful room to influence both the systems we build and the way we build them
* Work on a wide range of interesting problems, from internal apps and integrations to structured data workflows, experimentation, and AI-assisted tooling
* Partner with deeply cross-functional stakeholders across CX, Sales, Partnerships, Product, IT, and Platform teams, with visible impact on how Contentful operates internally

## What to expect?

This is a hands-on role with approximately 60% implementation and 40% architecture and planning

* Own and evolve a focused set of internal tools, utilities, and integrations that support the Customer Experience organization (CSMs, Support, Professional Services, Learning Services) and cross-functional internal users such as Sales, Partnerships, and Pricing & Packaging
* Lead R&D and rapid prototyping efforts to identify high-leverage solutions, then determine which ideas should remain lightweight, which should be operationalized, and which should be designed for clean handoff to other teams
* Build production-quality front-end and back-end code, including A

# Senior Software Engineer - Machine Learning (f/m/d)

London, England, United Kingdom

## **About the Opportunity**

**Become part of the AI revolution at Contentful where we build real world AI solutions for thousands of active customers.**  
At Contentful you will help imagine, drive, and build ML solutions which produce immediate customer impact by helping them get more done than they ever imagined. We are looking for an excellent  Senior Software Engineer - Machine Learning (f/m/d) who understands and has experience in this field and current Generative AI trends. If you're passionate about pushing the boundaries of what's achievable with ML products on a large scale content management system, we want you on our team.

## **What to expect?**

* Measure and ensure the high-quality output of ML workloads for our enterprise customers.
* Design, build, and measure production software in the Contentful Platform.
* Conduct focused research and testing using tools like Jupyter Notebook.
* Optimize generative AI products for accuracy, speed, and scalability.
* Integrate ML cloud solutions and adapt them to our large-scale use cases.
* Integrate prompt engineering and fine-tuning for customer use cases.
* Develop software that scales to meet the demands of high-load customer workloads.
* Lead design reviews with peers and stakeholders to decide amongst available technologies.
* Drive the ML education in the team and establish a high standard of quality & ownership

## **What you need to be successful?**

* Proven experience as a technical leader in a product software development environment
* Proven Track Record in Model Development, including successfully developing and deploying LLM-based models for various NLP tasks in real-world applications with production workloads
* Proven ability to work backwards from customer needs to deliver ML-based features that meet those needs
* Ability to organize and prioritize competing workloads
* Familiarity with distributed systems and cloud services (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP)
* Solid understanding of machine learning principles
* Experience with container frameworks such as Docker or Kubernetes
* Constructive Problem-Solving: As a natural problem-solver, you bring forward ideas that lead to practical solutions and contribute to product growth.
* Up-To-Date on The Latest Updates on LLM Development & Research: From RAGs to Open-Source models over Agent Architectures – you have an inherent drive to stay up-to-date on what is the latest and greatest

**Preferred:**

* Background includes machine learning and AI implementation
* You can translate a research paper into a PoC if the code does not exist

## **What’s in it for you?**

* Join an ambitious tech company reshaping the way people build digital experiences
* Full-time employees receive Stock Options for the opportunity to share in the success of our company
* Fertility and family building benefits, including a lifetime reimbursable wallet to support

# Senior Software Engineer

Barcelona

***🚀 We’re on a mission to make money work for everyone.***

We’re waving goodbye to the complicated and confusing ways of traditional banking.

After starting as a prepaid card, our product offering has grown a lot in the last 10 years in the UK. As well as personal and business bank accounts, we offer joint accounts, accounts for 16-17 year olds, a free kids account and credit cards in the UK, with more exciting things to come beyond. Our UK customers can also save, invest and combine their pensions with us.

With our hot coral cards and get-paid-early feature, combined with financial education on social media and our award winning customer service, we have a long history of creating magical moments for our customers!

We’re not about selling products - we want to solve problems and change lives through Monzo ❤️

---

📍Barcelona, Spain | 💰 Base salary for this role is depending on experience +Incentive Awards tied to your performance + benefits   
Hybrid model: 3 days a week in the office

**About our Engineering Teams:**

We have over 650 engineers across Monzo who design and build our in-house banking platform. We have big ambitions for the future, and technology plays a big role in creating a bank our customers want, so engineers at Monzo collaborate across disciplines to solve interesting challenges throughout the company.

These range from the products our customers use everyday to underlying infrastructure, security, payments and finance, customer operations, financial crime, and data, to name just a few areas. As a bank, there is scope for impact across a huge number of opportunities.

We contribute to **open source software** as much as possible. Our **blog**is a good place to learn even more about what we do!

**Your day-to-day**

This role is all about collaborating with your team to make a difference to your customers. As a backend engineer you’ll work in a squad alongside other disciplines like product managers, marketers, user researchers, designers, mobile engineers, web engineers, data analysts, business analysts, writers and more!

Together you’ll build and support a particular part of Monzo. Our squads belong to our wider *collectives* (a word we use to describe self-governing business units of ~100 people). They are; Core Banking, Business Banking, Wealth, Borrowing, Growth, Payments, Platform, Fincrime, Security & Expansion.

They’re all looking for additional Backend Engineers right now, we do a standard interview process across all our collectives and at the end we will find the best match for you based on your skills, experience, preferences and aligning with the business need!

Our backend engineers have a variety of different backgrounds. As long as you enjoy learning new things, we’d love to talk to you. We do not ask for formal qualifications or degree requirements for any of our engineering roles.

ABOUT YOU

## About our Product & Tech Department

Our mission is to digitize the classic shopping stroll, turning it into a personalized, inspiring experience for every customer. Technology and data sit at the center of everything we build. We are not a fashion company with a tech team on the side. Tech drives our product, our strategy, and our growth.  
Our proprietary tech stack powers our own shops and SCAYLE, our commerce infrastructure solution that serves over 350 brands globally. Same foundation, bigger stage. The challenges keep things interesting.

## What To Expect

* Develop and maintain high-quality, performant, and scalable frontend features for our Web Shop (Desktop, Mobile Web, Tablet) using React and TypeScript.
* Collaborate closely with product managers, UX designers, and QA managers to translate concepts and requirements into user-friendly and reliable features.
* Take an active role in solving complex technical problems and architecting innovative, sustainable frontend solutions.
* Take ownership of large-scale projects from conception to deployment, requiring little oversight.
* Foster a culture of high-quality code, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing.
* Actively participate in code reviews to ensure code quality, maintainability, and adherence to best practices.
* Contribute to optimizing our frontend architecture, performance (Core Web Vitals), and development workflows.

## Who You Are

* At least 4 yearsexperience with Frontend Development.
* Expert proficiency in JavaScript and TypeScript.
* Deep experience with React and its ecosystem (e.g. hooks, context).
* Strong skills in HTML and modern CSS (e.g. CSS-in-JS, responsive design).
* Solid understanding of frontend testing frameworks (e.g. Jest, React Testing Library).
* Experience integrating with REST APIs and managing application state (e.g., Redux, Zustand, React Query).
* Familiarity with build tools (e.g. Webpack, Vite) and package managers (e.g. npm, yarn).
* Strong knowledge of web performance optimization principles and accessibility (a11y).
* Proficient with Git and comfortable with CI/CD workflows.
* Excellent problem-solving: You can solve conceptual, non-standard problems and exercise solid judgment based on analyzing multiple sources of information.
* High autonomy and ownership: You are motivated, take matters into your own hands, and can lead a complex project or process with little oversight from your manager.

Nice to have

* Experience with server-side rendering (SSR).
* Experience working in a large-scale e-commerce environment.
* Knowledge of Node.js.
* Experience building or maintaining a design system or component library.
* Experience with end-to-end testing tools like Cypress.

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer, Personalization, Magenta | Life at Spotify
content:
# Senior Machine Learning Engineer

### Personalization, Magenta

Link copied to clipboard.

Apply now

Apply

Engineering

Machine Learning

New York

The Personalization team makes deciding what to play next easier and more enjoyable for every listener. From Blend to Discover Weekly, we’re behind some of Spotify’s most-loved features. We built them by understanding the world of music and podcasts better than anyone else. Join us and you’ll keep millions of users listening by making great recommendations to each and every one of them.

 The Personalization team at Spotify makes deciding what to listen to next feel effortless for hundreds of millions of users — from Discover Weekly to our newest AI-powered experiences. We’re now building conversational AI capabilities that let users interact with Spotify in natural language. You’ll join a squad working at the core of this space, shaping how users discover and engage with audio through intelligent, responsive systems.

What You'll Do

+ Design and ship production-grade machine learning systems powering conversational and agentic AI experiences
+ Build systems that interpret user intent, manage context across multi-turn interactions, and handle ambiguity reliably at scale
+ Develop and evolve agentic workflows including memory, context management, and multi-step tool orchestration
+ Create evaluation frameworks, including LLM-as-judge pipelines, to measure quality and guide iteration
+ Partner closely with product, engineering, and design to deliver seamless, user-facing experiences
+ Balance experimentation with production rigor, ensuring performance, latency, and reliability at Spotify scale
+ Continuously improve agent behavior through tight feedback loops between evaluation and real-world usage

Who You Are

+ You have 5+ years of experience building and shipping machine learning systems in production environments
+ You are experienced with large language models and have worked on real-world applications beyond experimentation; shipped and maintained large scale systems with LLMs
+ You have a deep understanding of challenges in conversational or agentic systems, such as context handling and multi-step reasoning
+ You know how to evaluate ML systems rigorously and have experience designing metrics or evaluation pipelines
+ You are comfortable debugging complex interactions between models, tools, and system constraints like latency
+ You care about building reliable, scalable systems that deliver high-quality user experiences
+ You enjoy working cross-functionally and contributing to a collaborative, inclusive team environment

Where You'll Be

+ This role is based in New York
+ We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

Learn about life at Spotify

title: Senior Frontend Engineer (m/f/d) | Jobs at Jedox GmbH
content:
# Senior Frontend Engineer (m/f/d)

Germany remote

Full-time

Permanent employee

## What is Jedox?

At Jedox, we offer cutting-edge Performance Management Platform and Solutions that enable businesses to plan, analyze, and optimize their financial and operational performance. Our B2B Platform-as-a-Service offering consists of various components - sophisticated data representation and data collection, a powerful planning engine, and data integration - developed by our specialized product teams.  
  
To drive our “Digital Business Partner” vision, we’re expanding our Engineering team and looking for a Full-Stack Software Engineer to help design and build the next generation of our platform services and applications.

## Your Responsibilities

As a Senior Frontend Engineer, you’ll design, build, and maintain scalable, cloud-ready web applications using React, TypeScript, and with micro-frontend architecture. You’ll take ownership across the modern frontends and system integrations - while driving code quality, CI/CD automation, and maintainable architecture. You’ll collaborate with Product Manager, UX/UI designer, QA in a cross-functional team to clarify requirements, align on priorities, and deliver value through efficient agile development. You’ll also mentor engineers through code reviews and knowledge sharing and contribute to cross-team technical initiatives that shape scalable, resilient systems.   
  
**What You’ll Do**

At Mollie, we’re on a mission to make payments and money management effortless for every business in Europe.

San Francisco, CA

Amplitude is the leading AI analytics platform, helping over 4,700 customers—including Atlassian, Burger King, NBCUniversal, and Square—build better products and digital experiences. With powerful AI Agents embedded across our platform, teams can analyze, test, and optimize user experiences faster than ever. Ranked #1 across multiple categories in G2’s Winter 2026 Report, Amplitude is the best-in-class solution for product, data, and marketing teams. Learn more at amplitude.com.

As an organization, we deliver for our customers by living our values. We operate from a place of humility, take ownership of problems and successes, approach challenges with a growth mindset, and put our customers at the center of everything we do.

**Amplitude’s Commitment to Diversity Equity & Inclusion (DEI):** Amplitude believes that diversity enables the creation of better products, improves the ability to solve complex problems, and drives more powerful solutions. We strive to create an environment of inclusion—one focused on psychological safety, empathy, and human connection—that will allow employees of all backgrounds to thrive.

## Senior Software Engineer; Analytics Compute Platform / Infrastructure Platform

## About the Role & Team

Every chart, insight, and experiment result a customer sees in Amplitude passes through the compute layer that this team owns. Our team sits between Amplitude's front-end analytics products/ rest APIs/ MCPs and Nova, our proprietary analytics database, and owns the compute API that translates a user's question into a fast, correct answer.

Our mandate: **enable a highly performant, reliable, and flexible way to compute insights.** Those three goals are often in tension the more flexible we make the system, the harder it is to keep it fast and simple, and a lot of the interesting engineering work on this team lives in that tradeoff.

Day to day, you'll work closely with engineers on data management, marketing analytics, Session Replay, Experiment, CDP, Query, and various product teams, since they're all consumers of what we build.

## As a Senior Software Engineer, you will

* Design and build core parts of the query/compute engine that powers analysis across Amplitude's product suite
* Evolve the compute API and semantic layer that other engineering teams build features on top of, so that it can support a more generic table
* Help unify and evolve our core data model, including how non-event data (profile properties, lookup tables, etc.) is represented alongside event data
* Improve the performance, reliability, and scalability of query planning and execution on the Amplitude query engine
* Partner with teams across Session Replay, Experiment, CDP, Query, and frontend to understand their needs and shape the compute layer around them
* Take ownership of projects end-to-end, from design through rollout

Ahoj! Welcome to Pipedrive Prague, a development center in the beautiful heart of Europe.

See open positions

## About us

Our Prague office is a dynamic engineering hub buzzing with diversity, culture and innovation. Home to 30+ talented teammates from more than ten countries, it’s a space designed to spark ideas and foster collaboration.

Movie-themed meeting rooms – including a Harry Potter fan favorite – make it a vibrant spot for team off-sites, attracting colleagues from across the globe.

Beyond work, we actively support Prague’s tech, data and HR communities, hosting regular meetups to share knowledge and inspire the next generation of talent.

## Our traditions

Our small, agile Prague team thrives on collaboration and innovation. A favorite tradition of ours is Lightning Talks, which began here in Prague to give people a safe, informal space to practice presenting. They’re optional, relaxed and always leave us feeling a little more inspired.

We take work-life balance seriously, with flexible hours that cater to night owls and early birds alike. Our office vibe is fun and welcoming, from foosball matches and ready-to-ride skateboards to carefully chosen coffee and tea, with a touch of Estonian flair from our slippers and sauna.

Connection is important at our Prague office, and we see it in the moments we share. Communal breakfasts, pickle-making and countless self-organized events capture the close-knit spirit that defines our team. We swap tips in our Prague-only Slack channels (prague-beauty, prague-investing and more), and when we clock out, you’ll find us belting out tunes during karaoke nights.

## Where you’ll find us

Our journey began in a co-working space, but in March 2020, we moved into a new office designed specifically for us. It’s located in a thriving Prague neighborhood – Karlín – and we’re sure we’re the first office in Prague with a sauna! We really enjoy the enchanting atmosphere here, which combines history and the pulse of modern business – complete with Harry Potter and Narnia-themed rooms that make work feel just a little more magical.

Our new home, Praga Studios, was recognized as one of the greenest buildings in the world. You can admire our beehives while drinking your coffee on the rooftop terrace with an amazing city view. Or, if you have green thumbs, try gardening right next to the building!

Pipedrive’s Tartu office opened in 2015 and is now home to one of our four engineering hubs, along with Tallinn, Prague and Lisbon. With 60+ employees representing more than 10 nationalities, we’re a tight-knit team with big ideas and even bigger energy.

While Pipedrive has grown into Estonia’s fifth unicorn, the culture in Tartu still carries the spirit of our early startup days – where creativity, humor and collaboration lead the way.  
Sure, their jokes are hilarious, but don’t let that fool you: “Tartu delivers” is this office’s motto, for a reason. The winning teams at our hackathons often come from here and the quality of their work speaks for itself.

Over the years, we’ve been recognized with multiple best employer awards in Estonia, but what makes this office truly special is its people. Whether you're a seasoned engineer or just starting your career, you'll find the space and support you need to thrive here.

## Our traditions

We like to keep things down to earth and fun. Whether that’s sharing self-organized lunches, competing in a bake-off, or cheering each other on during a Lightning Talk (where employees get to hone their presentation skills on a topic of their choice), there’s always something happening.

Each month, we come together for different activities – from sauna nights, board game battles and gaming tournaments to hiking, biking and even winter swimming. We’ve got a strong gaming culture, a love for the outdoors and a great sense of humor that ties it all together.

We also host our own version of Pipehikes, inspired by our team in Lisbon, and open the office doors to our little ones during PipeKids – an annual afternoon of creativity and play for employees’ families.  
 And at the end of the year, we join Pipedrivers around the world for our winter holiday celebration: an in-house award show full of recognition, fun sketches and community spirit, dubbed “The Pipeys”.

Whether you're leading a talk, jumping into a tournament or just relaxing in the sauna, there’s a place for you here.

## Where you’ll find us

Our Tartu office is located near the city center in the city’s first LEED Gold-certified office building – a modern, energy-efficient tower that reflects our commitment to a thoughtful work environment. We occupy two cozy, creative floors, giving us plenty of natural light and sweeping views of the Emajõgi river.

One is inspired by video games – yes, we game a lot – while the other is themed around Estonian birds. If you’re curious, yes, we really do enjoy pronouncing names like Ööbik and Tuttpütt.  
 We’ve created a welcoming space where everyone feels at home. Our slipper policy says it all. And with regular visits from our beloved office dogs (including Juuni, who even has her own Slack emojis), the vibe is friendly and relaxed.

# Florida

Come soak up the sun at our St. Pete office on the beautiful Central Gulf Coast.

See open positions

## About us

Welcome to our vibrant office in sunny St. Petersburg, Florida! We opened our doors in August 2019, and as our team has grown, we relocated in September 2023 to better serve our community and customers. With over 30 talented and dedicated employees, our Florida team plays a key role in sales, customer success, business development, workplace culture, and customer solutions.

## Our traditions

At Pipedrive, we believe that a happy team is a successful team. We’re a motivated and competitive group, and we keep our energy up with an endless supply of coffee and a fully stocked snack station – think savory bagels, noodles, fresh fruit and tasty granola bars. We also love to celebrate together! Whether it’s company updates, local holidays, or just because, you can count on us for delicious complimentary breakfasts and lunches and some delightful surprise treats.

When we’re not hard at work or indulging in snacks, we love to bond through fun group activities. From Topgolf and painting to bowling, VR games, and catching local sports events, there’s never a dull moment. Plus, we hold monthly in-office team days to help reconnect our remote employees and foster strong relationships. And as we close out the year, don’t miss our internal award show, where we celebrate our wonderful Pipedrivers with laughter and creative sketches featuring our kids, pets, and even customers!

## Where you’ll find us

Our office is nestled in beautiful downtown St. Petersburg, surrounded by lush greenery and offering stunning views of the Gulf. After work, you can easily explore a variety of fantastic restaurants, shops, and fun activities nearby. With 361 sunny days a year, why not take a leisurely stroll to enjoy the incredible art murals that St. Pete has to offer?

Inside, we’ve crafted an inspiring workplace that reflects Pipedrive’s lively culture. With an open-concept design, cozy breakout areas and vibrant decor, our office is designed to spark creativity and collaboration. Plus, many of our customers are just a stone’s throw away, giving us plenty of opportunities for face-to-face meetings in the office and around the area.

# New York

Welcome to the Big Apple! Our New York City office is located in the heart of Midtown Manhattan.

See open positions

## About us

Our first US office was in the Bay Area, but we moved to the East Coast in 2015 to be better connected to our European teams. Since then, New York has become the strategic hub for our US operations.

Today, you’ll find us in Midtown Manhattan with a close-knit team of over 15 Pipedrivers representing a range of departments. It’s a diverse and collaborative group, each person contributing their own perspective and experience.

The focus in New York leans more toward the business side – including sales, customer success, support and finance – and we all work together to keep things running smoothly and stay connected to our global mission.

## Our traditions

We believe strong teams are built both in and outside the office. From time to time, we take a break from our usual activities to share a meal, play a round of mini golf, see the New York Yankees play or volunteer together for causes we believe in.

One of our favorite annual highlights is Summer Garage – a company-wide event that brings Pipedrivers from around the world together for music, bonding and fun. And as the year wraps up, we celebrate during the winter holiday season with The Pipeys, our global award show where we recognize teammates who live our values every day.

No matter the event or season, we make time to enjoy each other’s company and create space for connection.

## Where you’ll find us

We’re located in the heart of Midtown Manhattan at 530 Fifth Avenue – just steps away from landmarks like Bryant Park, Times Square and Grand Central Station. The neighborhood is packed with great restaurants, culture and energy, offering something new to discover every day.

Our office is part of the Convene co-working space, which means we benefit from a range of shared amenities while enjoying the comfort of a private suite. The space accommodates up to 12 people and includes a meeting room and conference area. It may be compact, but there's plenty of space to work, collaborate and connect.

Whether you're grabbing lunch nearby or settling in with your laptop and coffee, you'll find everything you need to do your best work – right in the middle of it all.

## Meet our team

One of the things I value most about working at Pipedrive is the truly global collaboration. From my remote base in New York, I work closely with sales teams in Lisbon, London, Germany and Florida, and the connection between teams is seamless. The flexibility to work remotely while still having the option to spend time in the NYC office or visit colleagues in other hubs makes it a unique and rewarding place to grow. It’s the people and the supportive culture that make Pipedrive such a great place to work.

# Tallinn

Welcome to Tallinn, where Pipedrive was born.

Come and join Pipedrive’s Tallinn team!

See open positions

## About us

Pipedrive was born in Tallinn. What started as a small startup in a shared office has grown into an international company with an entire building in Tallinn’s vibrant WoHo quarter. Today, Tallinn is home to our engineering and product hub, where 300+ team members from 30+ nationalities work together – many of them engineers.

Pipedrive is proud to be Estonia’s fifth unicorn and has consistently been recognized as one of the best places in the country to work since 2016, according to both the media and our employees. And we’re not just about jobs – at Pipedrive, you’ll have the chance to grow, learn and make a real impact by helping shape a product used by more than 100,000 companies worldwide.

## Our traditions

At Pipedrive, we love to celebrate the moments that matter – from birthdays and work anniversaries to promotions. One of our most beloved traditions is getting together for *Kringle*, a classic Estonian pastry, though you’ll also see pizza, donuts or pancakes make an appearance. To make sure no celebration goes unnoticed, we’ve got a kitchen-alert Slack channel to keep everyone in the loop.

Our biggest annual event is the Summer Garage – a full-day celebration that brings teams together to connect, unwind and enjoy live music performed by our very own band (and sometimes a guest artist or two).

We also host occasional hackathons and innovation accelerators to spark new ideas for improving the product and how we work together.

We believe in a balance of fun and focus. From Lightning Talks where anyone can practice their presentation skills with a topic they care about, to Sudoku tournaments and gaming challenges organized by teammates, there’s always something to get involved in.

We’re also proud to contribute to the wider community: Our team actively supports the local engineering, product and HR scenes in Estonia by hosting in-house meetups, participating in conferences and speaking at events.

Staying active outside of work is also a big part of our culture. Whether it's a friendly game of table tennis, a disc golf match or a padel session – one of the most popular sports among our Tallinn team – there's always fun going on.

## Where you’ll find us

You’ll find us in Tallinn’s modern WoHo district, where our custom-designed office makes it easy to feel at home. Each of our eight themed floors – park, nature, sports, IT, music, art, movies and culture – is fully immersive. Whether you’re jamming on the music floor’s instruments or exploring the art gallery, there’s something for everyone.

The heart of the office is our all-hands area, where people gather for a coffee or something else from our well stocked kitchens. # Principal AI Engineer

We opened the doors of our Business Development Centre (BDC) in the heart of Hyderabad in December 2020. Young at heart, the BDC is a wonderful space that strengthens Backbase’s presence in the APAC region. The physical presence of great talent and support also makes way for long-term relationships with customers.

Nestled in the middle of Hyderabad, the state-of-the-art building sits amidst other global brands. The location offers stunning views of hills and lakes, while nearby there is a selection of restaurants, traditional bazaars, and other cultural hotspots.

Apply now

Backbase has entered a new era of digital banking with the launch of the world’s first AI-Native Banking OS. This is not just a platform; it is a fundamental shift in how banks operate. By unifying digital channels, front-office workspaces, and back-office operations under a single Control Plane, we enable banks to run as one. Our OS coordinates execution across customers, employees, and AI agents, ensuring every action is authorized, governed, and context-aware. At Backbase, we are transforming the promise of AI into measurable business impact for over 120 leading financial institutions globally.

The Principal AI Engineer - Banking OS Control Plane for Integrations is a pivotal role within our Ecosystems department. In this role, you won't just be using tools to build connectors; you will be the core engineer responsible for building and enhancing the Integrations Control Plane - Connector Studio—our proprietary, AI-powered platform designed to enable the seamless creation of connectors.

You will architect the intelligent engine and the platform tools that empower teams to integrate diverse banking systems effortlessly. You are building the "brain" and the machinery of the Connectivity and Integration Layer (powered by Grand Central), which acts as the critical bridge between our intelligent frontline and the complex world of legacy core systems, CRMs, and fintechs.

## What you'll do

We’re looking for a Principal AI Engineer with a passion and deep knowledge in building intelligent, context-aware agentic systems, tools, and platforms. You’ll play a key role in leading the development of the AI-powered studio, creating the intelligent workflows and automated tools that simplify and accelerate the generation of complex banking connectors.

🚀 Experienced: Minimum 8 years in software engineering, with 5+ years developing and delivering enterprise-grade AI applications.
